item.dc:creator: AllHailTheMoose

item.title: Laser Hair Removal Journey
item.description:
Hello Imgur! This month I am celebrating 1 year of laser hair treatment to zap away unwanted facial hair, and I wanted to share my journey with you! In this post I intend to be honest and as frank as possible for those of you who also might be considering laser hair removal. I have a double-whammy of hair-growing genes as I have PCOS (polycystic ovarian syndrome) which causes male-pattern hair growth, and I am also half-Arabic (and this is one ethnicity that has higher rates of male-pattern hair growth in females). Yay me! My facial hair became noticeably thick enough to need shaving when I was 12 years old. Initially I only needed to shave about ~1 per week, but as the years went by more frequent shaving was required. These last couple of years I have needed to shave my face at least twice a day, sometimes three times if I were going out with friends in the evening. It had reached a point where I was shaving in the morning before work, during lunch, and after work if going out in order to try to hide the fact that I had facial hair. The picture above is what my hair growth looked like after 1/2 day without shaving (before beginning laser hair removal).
This is what my facial hair looked like immediately after shaving (before beginning laser hair removal). I liked to believe it wasn't noticeable, but looking back I clearly had 5 o'clock shadow.
I NEVER let my face get to this point, BUT I was curious to see what I'd look like "with a beard" before beginning my laser hair journey. I got to 2 days before I couldn't stand it any longer. Think I could have pulled off an awesome Viking beard if I would have kept going? Maybe have a couple beard braids!
I began getting laser treatments in June of 2021. I have since had a treatment once every 5 weeks. They predicted I would need somewhere around 15 treatments to remove all of my facial hair as it was very thick. The picture above is 3 weeks after my first treatment. I was surprised and excited about how much hair didn't grow back! (disclaimer: I'm not sure if this is true or not, but it seems like the first treatment just freaks your body out a bit, because I definitely saw more hair between subsequent treatments, but it has since drastically reduced again)
They say the treatment is short enough that you can get it done "over your lunch break" and return to work the same day - UMMM NO! At least I wouldn't have! This is what my skin looked like immediately after treatment and would continue looking like this for several hours. When I get treated now it doesn't look quite so bad. It looked like the above picture for maybe the first ~6 - ~7 months. I started taking several ibuprophen pills beforehand to help with the swelling and discomfort (so maybe that's working?) Now you're probably wondering if it's painful. Well, if you've watched videos from various salons and clinics that perform the procedure, they're going to tell you that it feels like "a rubber band lightly snapping against your skin" or "a slight pinch". I think it really depends on the thickness of your hair follicles and the treatment location. I am also having my areolas treated, and I do not experience much pain there. But my face on the other hand...for me at least, it is quite painful. After my first treatment I had decided not to continue because the pain was so intense. I even had a couple nightmares about going back. I did go back though, and I bought a stress ball that looks like a seal to help get me through the procedure. I'm glad I continued with treatment. Although it hasn't gotten any less painful, it is worth noting that the pain does not last long. The procedure itself only takes about ~10 minutes, and by the time you're putting your shoes on you barely feel anything. The pain is almost entirely isolated to the instant the laser is used and the seconds after.
They will tell you to "EXFOLIATE! EXFOLIATE! EXFOLIATE!" after every treatment in order to scrub the dead hairs out! Well, I took this seriously and was a little over-zealous about it, and ended up scrubbing off several layers of skin. I had a nasty scab for a couple weeks...
This is ~11 months into treatment! I had not shaved my chin for FIVE WEEKS (I had shaved my upper lip)! If you zoom in, you can see some long dark hairs around my jawline, but they are thin and wispy! PROGRESS!!!
This picture was taken today. This is just a bit over 1 year into treatment. I had not shaven for 1 week! (not my chin NOR my upper lip!) See those few hairs? Just a few of them? No more 5 o'clock shadow! No more daily shaving! I still have a few more treatments to go, and I am so glad I stuck with it! So much time has been saved in the morning getting ready for work. No more accidently nicking my skin and claiming to have a "bad pimple that bled" there. No more worrying about how I'm going to shave while traveling. It's truly a freeing feeling.
Cat tax! This is Benji! :)
Double cat tax! This is Iris! :D
TRIPLE CAT TAX! This is Benji and Iris together =^_^= TLDR: - I had a hairy face (and bewbies) - Got zapped every 5 weeks for a year (ow it hurts!) - Lost a lot of hair! - Still have a few more zappy zaps to get - Worth it! Also: I'm being treated by Milan Laser Hair Removal instead of a salon. I felt it would be safer. In total I spent $2,907 for my face, and $893.76 for my areolas. These are one time payments that cover an unlimited # of treatments for these two areas for life.

item.title: FYI
item.description: Useful tip from Tyrrell Auto Centers, Most all vehicles have a recirculation button with the AC controls in their cars. But many of us are unsure when to use it. Well, the easy answer is to use it in the summer and turn it off in the winter. The recirculation button simply takes the air from inside the car and recirculates it in the cabin instead of pulling fresh air from outside. On days like today when it is miserably hot outside, if you do not recirculate the cooler air in the cabin, than your AC system is pulling hot air from outside and trying to cool it. Using the recirculation feature will get your car cooler and will decrease the wear and tear on your AC system. - Side note, if your car has been baking in the sun, its better to roll the windows down and turn recirculate off for the first minute or so to get rid of the super hot air inside the car before turning the recirculate on. Also, any time you are stuck in traffic ( summer or winter) be sure to use the recirculate. If you are pulling air from outside, then you are pulling in all the pollutants and carbon monoxide from all the traffic. Studies show that recirculating your AC can cut down on the pollutants entering your vehicle by 20% when stuck in traffic!

item.title: My cat crossed the rainbow bridge today
item.description:
My naym is Cass I was da best Da bestest kat Above the rest
I liked to play And eat da fudz And also sleep Wif my best bud
Wen I was lil I loved to eat But whate'er you do Don't touch my peets
I had nicknames Like furball face And peanut, too And just in case
You try to cry Or miss my ass Remember good stuff My naym was Cass
item.dc:creator: nkschum75

item.title: This makes me Happy, great explanation.
item.description: #advice_animals #alberta I might need to rehome my fur babies Olive and Sid, they’re ferrets and have bonded for years. I’ve had ferrets for 7 years, these two are great and got them as previous ones got older as every one knows “everyone needs a friend”. I hate to rehome them, but I want to make sure they have a roof over they’re head and are loved and warm. Due to Covid and stuff, the ferret rescue isn’t running I could also use the money for a private adoption. Help make sure there is a place for me to sleep. Mostly I just want to make sure they are in a home they are loved, if you know of anyone who is the an animal lover or know of trusted rehome/relinquish with good established repor. Their my babies and care. Had to make a new account as I can’t recover the password for my other account. @billybooya

item.title: Fanny, one of the first all-female rock groups to achieve critical and commercial success
item.description: Active in the early to mid 1970s. David Bowie once said about Fanny: “One of the most important female bands in American rock has been buried without a trace. And that is Fanny. They were one of the finest fucking rock bands of their time, in about 1973. They were extraordinary: They wrote everything, they played like motherfuckers, they were just colossal and wonderful, and nobody’s ever mentioned them. They’re as important as anybody else who’s ever been, ever; it just wasn’t their time. Revivify Fanny. And I will feel that my work is done.” The band was founded by a Filipina-American guitarist named June Millington and her sister, bassist Jean. Alice de Buhr on drums, and Nickey Barclay on the keyboard. A cover of "Hey Bulldog" by the Beatles

item.title: My company has restructured our bonuses "for our benefit" by reducing our bonuses and raising our wage... by less than they reduced our bonuses
item.description: Text message chain with my boss. And it's actually even worse than this, we all got together and built a spreadsheet of our takehome compared to the new proposal, I'm not losing $800 a year, I'm losing $1500. At hourly, it comes out to just over two weeks of unpaid work every year. We're organizing a walkout and talking about unionizing. Edit for clarity- I am aware you shouldn't count a bonus as part of the pay, our beef is that based off last years tax documents, the new MAX bonus is less than half of the AVERAGE bonuses we all recieved last year. They raised my salary by $4,000, but cut my potential average bonus by $5,560
